query: How likely is regional sea level to exceed 0.6m by 2070 in our coastal district?

questions.question: Which emissions trajectory do you want to anchor on (SSP2-4.5, SSP5-8.5)?
questions.why: Scenario choice swings the central projection by tens of cm.

questions.question: Should regional subsidence be modelled separately?
questions.why: Many coastal sites have local subsidence that adds linearly to global SLR.

components.name: Global mean sea-level rise by 2070
components.description: IPCC central projection for the chosen SSP trajectory.
components.why: Anchors regional projection; widest single source of variance.

components.name: Regional amplification factor
components.description: Ocean dynamics scaling factor relative to global mean for this coast.
components.why: Bounded but uncertain; triangular captures the most-likely-with-extremes shape.

components.name: Local subsidence rate (mm/year)
components.description: Long-term local land subsidence; integrated over the 45-year horizon.
components.why: Adds linearly to relative SLR; well-constrained by tide-gauge records.

components.name: Ice-sheet rapid-collapse contribution
components.description: Low-probability, high-impact tail from Antarctic ice instability.
components.why: Right-skewed tail; the variance lives in the upper tail.

global_slr_mean.reasoning: IPCC AR6 SSP2-4.5 reports 0.32-0.45m likely range by 2070; using midpoint and CI-to-SD.

regional_amplification.reasoning: Regional ocean dynamics typically amplify mid-latitude coastal SLR by 5-30% above global mean.

subsidence_rate.reasoning: Tide-gauge analysis for similar coastal districts typically reports 1.5-3 mm/yr subsidence.

ice_sheet_collapse_tail.reasoning: Low-probability tail contribution; central is small but upper tail is the main risk.
